title: SonicWall Cloud SWG and SMA Access Events (SonicWall)
spl:
index=proxy sourcetype="dell:sonicwall:firewall" earliest=-7d
| where fw_action IN ("block","deny","drop")
| stats count by cat, usr, fw_action, rule
| sort -count
description: SonicWall Cloud SWG and Secure Mobile Access (SMA) provide cloud-delivered web security and ZTNA for remote and branch users. Tracking blocked connections, authentication events, and policy actions validates secure access enforcement.
value: VPN operations teams monitor SonicWall SMA session health, detecting brute force patterns, credential sharing through concurrent sessions from multiple IPs, and abandoned sessions consuming VPN licenses.
implementation: Forward SonicWall Cloud SWG/SMA logs via syslog or use the SMA 1000 TA. Map `cat` (web category) to acceptable-use policy. Alert on authentication failures from SMA. Report on policy enforcement effectiveness.
visualization: Bar chart (blocks by category), Table (top blocked users), Line chart (daily block trend), Pie chart (action distribution).
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic
  by All_Traffic.user, All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest span=1h
| sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Access denied for users in mid-onboarding, role changes pending propagation, or VDI sessions with new device IDs.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• SonicWall SMA (Secure Mobile Access) logs in `index=sonicwall` with `sourcetype=sonicwall:sma`. Key fields: `msg_id` (SMA-specific message IDs), `user`, `src`, `dst`, `action`, `tunnel_type` (SSL-VPN/NetExtender/Mobile Connect), `auth_method`, `duration`, `bytes_in`, `bytes_out`.
• SonicWall SMA appliances provide SSL-VPN access for remote workers. NetExtender is the full-tunnel client; Mobile Connect is for phones/tablets. SMA supports MFA and device posture checks.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Configure syslog on SMA: System > Log > Syslog Servers > add Splunk syslog input.

Verify:
```spl
index=sonicwall sourcetype="sonicwall:sma" earliest=-24h
| stats count by msg_id, action
```

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Primary search — VPN session monitoring:
```spl
index=sonicwall sourcetype="sonicwall:sma" earliest=-24h
| where match(msg_id, "(?i)login|logon|session")
| stats count(eval(match(action, "(?i)success|allow"))) as successful count(eval(match(action, "(?i)fail|deny"))) as failed dc(src) as source_ips by user
| eval fail_rate=round(failed/(successful+failed)*100, 1)
| sort - fail_rate
```

Understanding this SPL: Tracks VPN login success/failure per user. High failure rates may indicate brute force attacks (many failures, many source IPs) or credential issues (one user, one source, many failures). SonicWall SMA msg_id codes identify specific events.

Concurrent session anomaly:
```spl
index=sonicwall sourcetype="sonicwall:sma" earliest=-24h
| where match(msg_id, "(?i)session.*start|login.*success")
| transaction user maxpause=1h
| where mvcount(src) > 1
| table user, src, duration, tunnel_type
```

Step 3 — Validate
(a) Connect via NetExtender. Verify login event.
(b) In SMA: Log > View All. Compare with Splunk.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard: Row 1 — VPN sessions. Row 2 — Failed logins. Row 3 — Concurrent sessions.
Alert: Brute force (>10 failures in 5 min from same source).

Runbook:
1. **Brute force** — Block source IP in SMA firewall rules.
2. **Concurrent sessions** — Credential sharing or compromise.
3. **High duration** — Check for abandoned sessions consuming licenses.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **No SMA events** — Syslog may not be configured on SMA appliance.
• **`msg_id` numeric** — Map SonicWall message IDs using the SonicWall documentation.
grandmaExplanation: SonicWall provides a secure tunnel for people working from home. We watch for signs of trouble — like someone's password being guessed by attackers, or the same login being shared between multiple people, which is both a policy violation and a security risk.
hardware: SonicWall SMA 1000 series, SonicWall Cloud SWG, SonicWall NSa/NSsp (with SASE), SonicWall Cloud Edge
